entities.name: RICE
entities.description: The crop affected by sheath blight disease and the target for developing resistance.

entities.name: SHEATH BLIGHT
entities.description: A devastating fungal disease caused by Rhizoctonia solani that leads to severe loss in rice grain production.

entities.name: RHIZOCTONIA SOLANI
entities.description: The fungal pathogen responsible for causing sheath blight disease in rice.

entities.name: THANATEPHORUS CUCUMERIS
entities.description: The telemorph form of the fungal pathogen Rhizoctonia solani.

entities.name: RNA INTERFERENCE
entities.description: A biological process in which RNA molecules inhibit gene expression or translation, used here to silence pathogen genes.

entities.name: RNASEQ
entities.description: A sequencing-based technique used to analyze the transcriptome and identify RNAi target genes.

entities.name: TRANSCRIPTOME SEQUENCING
entities.description: A method used to analyze the complete set of RNA transcripts to study pectin metabolism associated genes.

entities.name: PECTIN
entities.description: A structural heteropolysaccharide used as a growth medium for R. solani and targeted by degrading enzymes.

entities.name: AG1IA_04727
entities.description: A key candidate gene in R. solani that encodes the polygalacturonase enzyme.

entities.name: POLYGALACTURONASE
entities.description: A pectin-degrading enzyme encoded by the gene AG1IA_04727 and upregulated during infection.

entities.name: DISEASE RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of a rice cultivar to resist infection by the sheath blight pathogen.

entities.name: PATHOGEN-DERIVED RESISTANCE
entities.description: A strategy for developing resistance in crops by targeting essential genes of the pathogen using RNAi.

entities.name: FUNGICIDES
entities.description: Chemical agents used to manage sheath blight disease in the absence of resistant cultivars.

relationships.description: used to analyze pectin metabolism genes of
